scenario_description: A large sourdough loaf is baked, sliced, and toasted with butter across three turns, while a smaller rye loaf sits untouched in the background of every shot. The final question requires distinguishing which specific loaf underwent the toasting process.

turns.text: Just pulled this beauty out of the oven. Looks like it rose well.
turns.image_description: A close-up shot of a large, golden-brown round loaf of sourdough bread sitting on a wire cooling rack. The crust has a deep amber color with distinct flour dusting and a jagged score mark on top. In the blurred background to the right, a smaller, dark brown rectangular loaf of rye bread sits on a wooden cutting board. The lighting is warm and natural, coming from a window on the left.

turns.text: That looks incredible! The crust on that sourdough is perfect. Is that rye in the background for later?
Yeah, slicing up the big one now. Thick slices for sandwiches.
turns.image_description: The same large sourdough loaf from the previous image is now sliced into four thick pieces on a white marble countertop. The interior crumb is visible, showing an open, airy texture with irregular holes. The small dark rye loaf remains untouched on the wooden board in the background, slightly out of focus. A serrated knife rests next to the sliced sourdough.

turns.text: Those slices look substantial. What are you planning to do with them?
Here's the finished product. I added some butter.
turns.image_description: Two thick slices of bread are sizzling in a stainless steel frying pan on a stovetop. The bread is golden and crispy, topped with melting pats of yellow butter. The texture and crumb pattern match the large sourdough loaf seen previously. In the far background, the small dark rye loaf is still sitting on the counter, completely whole and untoasted.

turns.text: Looks delicious! That butter is melting perfectly.
Which of the two loaves visible in the kitchen was the one I toasted with butter? A) The large golden sourdough B) The small dark rye C) Both were toasted D) Neither was toasted

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 introduces two entities: a large golden sourdough and a small dark rye. Turn 3 shows the large sourdough being sliced while the rye remains whole. Turn 5 shows bread being toasted in a pan; visual inspection of the crumb and crust confirms it matches the large sourdough, while the small rye is visible in the background of the same image, remaining whole and untouched. Therefore, only the large sourdough was toasted.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the identity of the 'large golden sourdough' across three different visual contexts (whole, sliced, toasted) while ignoring the persistent but inactive distractor (the small rye loaf). A model focusing only on the final image might struggle if it cannot link the crumb texture back to the first turn, or might be confused by the presence of the rye loaf in the background of the final image.

_meta.scenario: A large loaf of sourdough bread is shown fresh from the oven, then sliced into thick pieces, and finally toasted with butter in a separate pan. A second, smaller loaf of rye bread is visible in the kitchen throughout but never touched.
